title: marsdenia macrocalyx
intro: Marsdenia macrocalyx is a perennial climbing plant native to tropical Asia, belonging to the Apocynaceae family, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. The plant is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Marsdenia macrocalyx include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of Marsdenia macrocalyx can be made by drying the roots and leaves, which can then be used to make teas, decoctions, or infusions for medicinal purposes.
